hypothesis: If 3D molecule generation models are trained on a Kekulized GEOM-Drugs dataset with corrected aromatic valencies and evaluated using a GFN2-xTB geometry protocol, then they will produce molecules with higher chemical validity and significantly lower Δr, Δθ, Δφ, and ΔE_relax than models trained on the original dataset and evaluated with MMFF94 because the corrections remove valency-induced errors and xTB more accurately reflects quantum-consistent energetics.
